Analy Ɵcs (HFA)
Based on Systema Ɵc AcƟve InvesƟng

Global risks have intensiﬁed following the coordinated military ac Ɵons in Iran, triggering a surge in crude prices and placing the downward trajectory of global interest rates under signiﬁcant pressure. This geopoli Ɵcal strife, the perceived closure of the Strait of Hormuz, coupled with US Supreme Court's decision on tariﬀs have reignited inﬂa Ɵon concerns and driven a ﬂight to safe havens. Consequently, we are seeing a cyclical uptrend in the DXY and renewed momentum in Gold and Silver ETFs, which will likely be short lived.

The mid-February “SaaSpocalypse” — a violent rota Ɵon out of technology and global IT services—further underscores the vulnerability of developed market equi Ɵes, where complacency indicators remain at historic highs and corporate earnings are increasingly met with selling pressure.

In stark contrast, the Indian equity market remains a “Structural Bull-run,” bolstered by a superior compounding GDP outlook that comfortably outpaces that of other EM na Ɵons. DomesƟc stability is supported by a prudent ﬁscal consolida Ɵon path and a favorable inﬂexion point in monetary policy, with the Reserve Bank of India indica Ɵng an extended period of low rates. We believe that global investors are underes ƟmaƟng such structural and decisive moves.

The recent agreement with the US to reduce tariﬀs on Indian imports to 18%, and further developments, poten Ɵally reducing them to 15% or lower, provide decisive tailwinds. Trade with the US provides phenomenal beneﬁt to India, because our human produc Ɵvity is maximized and ﬁnancial costs are op Ɵmized, beƩer than with any other naƟon or region in the world. To further bolster ﬁscal prudence, the government also launched the NMP 2.0 asset mone ƟzaƟon plan aims to unlock massive value from public infrastructure.
Crucially for the discerning investor, while global markets face price-to-earnings contrac Ɵon, India's rela Ɵve valuaƟons have corrected and now align with historical macroeconomic averages.

In this climate of global fragmenta Ɵon, we believe value will outperform growth. We advise maintaining a focus on high-liquidity stocks where safety and fundamental strength remain the most cri Ɵcal priori Ɵes for capital preservaƟon and growth.

The core engine that drives us and sets us apart is a robust and diﬀeren Ɵated investment framework that enables us to
see beyond the horizon and stay relevant
Our unique analy Ɵcal framework for enabling ‘pr edic Ɵv e analy Ɵcs’ encompasses all available asset classes and sectors,
formula Ɵng a mulƟ-dimensional research per spec Ɵve.
Why mul Ɵ-dimensional?

Because the markets are a complex, dynamic system. There is no one formula or strategy or perspec Ɵve that can consistently outperform. A diverse set of variables and par Ɵcipants are conƟnuously inter ac Ɵng with each other in a
myriad ways.
In the face of this uncertainty and complexity, we have found consistent success by studying markets along six dimensions
as opposed to limi Ɵng ourselves to any one school of thought: Market Microstructure, Alternate Data, Risk, Cycles,
Objec Ɵvity and VolaƟlity.
The pursuit of risk management in today's markets demands more than selec Ɵve in tui Ɵon or episodic con vic Ɵon. It
calls for a process that is both structured and adap Ɵve, one that can absorb complexity without being paralysed by it.
Systema Ɵc AcƟve InvesƟng is that process — an investment style that combines the structural discipline of passive
inves Ɵng with the adaptability and insight of discreƟonary acƟve management.
At its core, it is a structured, rules-based decision architecture that is both convic Ɵon-driven and risk-aware. It leverages
machine intelligence, advanced analy Ɵcs, and human insight to idenƟf y opportuni Ɵes across asset classes, construct
resilient por ƞolios, and manage risks with precision. This style thrives on dat a density and analy Ɵcal depth,
con Ɵnuously interrogaƟng high-frequency signals, structural dislocaƟons, and behavioural anomalies across markets.
At quant, Systema Ɵc AcƟve InvesƟng forms the strategic backbone of qsif , enabling dynamic posi Ɵoning across
decisions originate from measurable signals — derived from price behaviour, market microstructure and macro cycles
with real- Ɵme data integraƟon, and mulƟ-factor modelling. Unlike convenƟonal acƟve invesƟng , which o Ōen depends
on episodic human judgment or passive inves Ɵng, which forgoes responsiveness, this style operates within a
disciplined, repeatable and adap Ɵve framework.
In essence, Systema Ɵc AcƟve InvesƟng is the operaƟng system that powers our long-short con vic Ɵon: a high-integrity,
low-latency, mul Ɵ-layered architecture that seeks to deliver asymmetric returns with insƟtuƟonal robustness.
Systema Ɵc AcƟve InvesƟng
Financial markets are no longer a simple reﬂec Ɵon of real economic output. They have evolved into hyper connected,

mul Ɵ-layered systems where liquidity, senƟment, and cross-asset correlaƟons create comple x and o Ōen nonlinear dynamics. The periodic disloca Ɵons witnessed in recent decades — whether triggered by central bank pivots, sovereign defaults, pandemics, or technological shocks — illustrate that the health of the system is as much a func Ɵon of liquidity
ﬂow as of underlying produc Ɵvity.
Our investment framework MARCOV addresses this by integra Ɵng high-frequency indicators through our High Frequency Analy Ɵcs (HFA).
At its founda Ɵon lies a rec ogni Ɵon that markets are not conƟnuous streams but layered tapestries of liquidity and intent.

HFA reads this fabric at its highest resolu Ɵon, drawing upon trade-level data, depth dynamics, liquidity gradients, sen Ɵmen t dri Ōs, and volaƟlity clusters to reveal the subtle inﬂexion points that precede regime change. Its architecture is

designed for seamless throughput and immediate response, yet its purpose is enduring: to enhance strategic posture without distor Ɵng structural intent. Through advanced state-mapping, HFA quanƟﬁes order ﬂow toxicity, tracks liquidity
fractures, and an Ɵcipates adver se selec Ɵon long before it manifests in convenƟonal frames. It aligns ex ecu Ɵon with

natural liquidity rhythms, using decay curves and transac Ɵon cost intelligence to minimise footprint without compromising convic Ɵon. VolaƟlity clustering models further reﬁne exposure bands, ensuring that agility does not come
at the cost of stability, and that drawdown containment coexists with convex upside capture.
Within the MARCOV framework for qsif, HFA acts as the Ɵming oracle—the r eal- Ɵme intelligence layer that synchronises

predic Ɵve signals with market reality. It is the bridge between the model and the moment, allowing strategies to ﬂex without losing coherence, and por ƞolios to move with precision rather than haste. HFA is a living system of observaƟon and adapta Ɵon, enabling decisiv e ac Ɵon under uncertainty while maintaining ﬁdelity to long-term design. By doing so, it
allows for the iden ƟﬁcaƟon of inﬂexion points not visible through tr adi Ɵonal quarterly or annual datasets, and for a
con Ɵnuous recalibraƟon of porƞolio exposures across equity, debt, derivaƟves, and alternate assets.
High Frequency Analysis (HFA)
quantamine is a fully integrated, in-house intelligence and execu Ɵon architecture engineered for latency -sensi Ɵv e mul Ɵ-
asset strategies. Designed as the central nervous system of the ﬁrm’s investment opera Ɵons, it uniﬁes risk, compliance,
investments, and opera Ɵons int o a fric Ɵonless, coordinated workﬂow. It ingests heterogeneous, high-dimensional
datasets on macroeconomy, microstructure, sen Ɵment, liquidity, and volaƟlity int o a single ac Ɵonable layer. Its
architecture u Ɵlizes advanced paƩern rec ogni Ɵon models to detect r egime shi Ōs, liquidity stress points, and
microstructure anomalies, dynamically recalibra Ɵng risk exposure in r eal- Ɵme.
Born in 1995 as Stockmagic on a personal computer at the Founder’s residence, the pla ƞorm grew into a large-scale eﬀort
employing 75 engineers at its peak. Its evolu Ɵon has been forged in crises: Risk AppeƟt e Analy Ɵcs emerged aŌer the 2000
dot-com collapse to gauge shi Ōs in investor toler ance, Liquidity Analy Ɵcs developed during the 2008 ﬁnancial crisis to
track hidden fragili Ɵes in funding markets, and Mone y Flow Analy Ɵcs was synthesized as their culminaƟon to map cross-
asset capital movements. Post the 2020 COVID-19 crash, Percep Ɵon AnalyƟcs reoriented from staƟc earnings forecasts to
modelling valua Ɵon mulƟples, while VolaƟlity AnalyƟcs expanded across asset classes to anƟcipate regime breaks and
bolster proac Ɵve risk management.
These pillars now form a Ɵghtly interlink ed, adap Ɵve framework that allows quantamine to anƟcipate market change with
precision instead of simply reac Ɵng to it. Alongside, the plaƞorm delivers custom dashboards and perf ormance analy Ɵcs
at any level of granularity. Extensive logging, maker-checker controls, and breach tracking ensure an auditable
environment that balances agility and governance.
quantamine pla ƞorm objec Ɵvity is our religion, data is god

sections.title: RISK BAND

subsections.title: March's equity market outlook is heavily inﬂuenced by the latest geopoli Ɵcal strife emanaƟng out of Iran and the apparent closure of the Strait of Hormuz, coupled with US Supreme Court's decision on tariﬀs, which have reignited crude-linked global inﬂa Ɵon concerns and driven a ﬂight to safe havens, driving the dollar higher. In the very near-term, market sen Ɵment could remain fragile with elevated risk aversion. During such a phase, we maintain a posi Ɵve bias with opportunisƟc short posi Ɵons, while behavioural indicators point to a climac Ɵc phase of capitulaƟon, even as the precise Ɵming of a reversal remained diﬃcult to idenƟfy.

Longer term, we con Ɵnue to maintain a cons truc Ɵve outlook. Several pivotal catalysts for posi Ɵve market senƟment have already emerged, most notably the ﬁnaliza Ɵon of trade agreements between India and both the European Union and the United States. These milestones, complemented by favourable budgetary provisions and the enduring strength of macroeconomic indicators, underscore a robust economic environment. While the market has yet to demonstrate an immediate reac Ɵon, these developments validate the assessment that the underlying structural trajectory remains sound. Consequently, there remains signiﬁcant poten Ɵal for market sen Ɵment and price performance to eventually converge with these fundamental drivers.

The bigger picture for Indian equi Ɵes remain cons truc Ɵve, driven by signs of an earnings cycle bo Ʃoming, a peaking USDINR, suppor Ɵve macroeconomic c ondi Ɵons, rotaƟon of ﬂows from developed markets to emerging markets, and exhausƟon in FPI selling.

During the month, the fund maintained an average equity deployment (Long + short) of approximately 95.43%, average long exposure: ~77%, average short exposure: ~11%, average net exposure: ~66%, month-end por ƞolio beta: 0.51.

sections.title: RISK BAND
DAILY FUND EXPOSURE

Indian equi Ɵes remain within a long-term structural bull market, but the recent phase has been par Ɵcularly challenging for small and mid-cap stocks. Given the extreme geopoli Ɵcal situaƟon in the Middle East, suscep Ɵble pockets of the SMID universe may suﬀer sharp reac Ɵons upon further negaƟve developments and earnings disappointments.

High-quality small and mid-cap franchises will con Ɵnue to be core to the por ƞolio, complemented by selec Ɵve t ac Ɵcal hedges and opportunis Ɵc short posi Ɵons. Behavioural and posi Ɵoning indicators increasingly pointed to a capitulaƟon- like phase within parts of the SMID space, although an inﬂexion point is hard to iden Ɵfy at this stage.

Looking beyond the near-term, we remain construc Ɵve on the medium-term outlook for small and mid-caps. The trade agreements between India and both the European Union and the United States aside, any resolu Ɵon of the conﬂict in the Middle East will augur well for the reversion of the ﬂow of global risk capital back to Indian SMIDs. The underlying trajectory of the space is structurally sound and eventually, market sen Ɵment and price ac Ɵon are w ell-posi Ɵoned to eventually align with these strong fundamentals. Favourable trade and budgetary provisions and the strengthening macroeconomic indicators underscore a robust economic environment for the country.

During the month, the fund maintained an average equity deployment (long + short) of approximately 89%, average long exposure: ~78%, average short exposure: ~5%, average net exposure: ~73%, month-end por ƞolio beta: 0.75.

subsections.title: Hybrid por ƞolios such as the qsif Hybrid Long-Short fund have the potenƟal to match up to a sensi Ɵve and volaƟle environment as current, with their balanced posi Ɵoning and diversiﬁcaƟon. As equity markets swing and gyrate with rising volaƟlity, debt and yield-generaƟng instruments provided stability and predictability. The fresh round of geopoli Ɵcal tensions out of the Middle East, the apparent closure of the Strait of Hormuz, coupled with US Supreme Court's decision on tariﬀs have reignited inﬂa Ɵon concerns and driven a ﬂight to safe haven instruments. As global risks have intensiﬁed, upwards risk for inﬂa Ɵon has risen and the downward trajectory of global interest rates has been put under signiﬁcant pressure. However, in ﬁxed income, suppor Ɵve macro c ondi Ɵons and contained inﬂaƟon have helped maintain stability in return proﬁle. Income-oriented assets, including InvITs con Ɵnued to contribute predictable cash ﬂows, reinforcing por ƞolio stability even as equity swings increased. Arbitrage opportuni Ɵes, debt and yield-focused instruments conƟnue to anchor the por ƞolio, allowing investors to beneﬁt from both capital growth and income genera Ɵon, without taking undue risk. Longer term, both debt and equity asset classes seem to be in a sweet spot. Equity market valua Ɵons have corrected, realigning with historical averages. The government has oﬃcially launched NMP 2.0 to unlock public asset value, while the corporate earnings cycle appears to have bo Ʃomed out following the September quarter. Central bank support suggests the USDINR exchange rate is stabilizing rather than ini ƟaƟng a structural breakout. February's Union Budget maintained a prudent ﬁscal consolidaƟon path. Monetary policy has reached a favourable inﬂexion point, with the R.B.I. Governor indica Ɵng that interest rates will likely remain low for an extended duraƟon. During the month, the fund maintained an average equity deployment (long + short) of approximately 96%, average long exposure: ~29%, average short exposure: ~9%, average net exposure: ~63%, month-end por ƞolio beta: 0.07.

sections.title: ASSET ALLOCATION
How to read qsif Factsheet

Investment Objec Ɵve: Investment Objec Ɵve states the purpose of the fund and the nature of returns it seeks to generate over the long term, as deﬁned in the Investment Strategy Informa Ɵon Document (ISID).
Fund Size: Fund Size, also referred to as Assets Under Management (AUM), represents the total market value of investments managed by the fund as on the stated date.
Monthly Fund Commentary: Monthly Fund Commentary provides the fund manager's perspec Ɵve on market c ondi Ɵons and summarizes key por ƞolio ac Ɵons undertaken during the month.

Daily Long-Short Equity Exposure (%): This sec Ɵon reﬂects the daily equity exposure of the fund, including long exposure (posi Ɵons that beneﬁt from an increase in prices) and short exposure (posi Ɵons that beneﬁt from a decrease in prices).

Daily Por ƞolio Beta: Por ƞolio Beta measures the volaƟlity of the fund rela Ɵve to the market. A beta lower than one indicates lower vola Ɵlity than the market, while a beta higher than one indicates higher vola Ɵlity.

Por ƞolio beta based on NAV returns can lag actual market exposure, par Ɵcularly in ac Ɵvely managed strategies involving deriva Ɵves or long–short posiƟoning. We calculate beta bo Ʃom-up using the daily equity holdings. To beƩer capture current market sensi Ɵvity, individual stock betas are esƟmated using an exponen Ɵally weighted moving average (EWMA), which gives greater weight to recent price movements and adapts faster than tradi Ɵonal historical averages. Widely used by ac Ɵve equity and hedge strategies globally, it is well suited for ac Ɵvely managed por ƞolios evolving exposures. A variance ﬂoor based on long-term benchmark vola Ɵlity is applied to avoid distor Ɵons during low-volaƟlity periods. The Ni Ōy 500 Total Return Index is used as a broad-market reference to ensure stock and por ƞolio betas are consistent and comparable across funds. Investors should interpret this beta as a point-in- Ɵme measure of market exposure rather than a return forecast, and as one input in assessing overall por ƞolio risk.

About the Investment Strategy: This sec Ɵon describes the manner in which the fund proposes to invest its corpus across various instruments and strategies in order to achieve its stated investment objec Ɵve, within applicable regulatory limits.
Investment Style: Investment Style indicates the broad approach adopted by the fund.
Incep Ɵon Date: Incep Ɵon Date refers to the ﬁrst date of allotment of units to the investors of the fund.
Rela Ɵve Weights: Rela Ɵve Weights show the diﬀerence between the fund's exposure and the benchmark exposure across securi Ɵes or segments, indicaƟng areas of overweight or underweight posi Ɵoning.
Asset Alloca Ɵon: Asset Alloca Ɵon shows how the fund's investments are distributed across asset classes such as equity, debt, and other instruments as on the stated date.
Contribu Ɵon by Market Cap: This sec Ɵon displays the allocaƟon of equity investments across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap companies.
Fund Managers: Fund Managers are employees of the asset management company responsible for managing the investments of the fund.

Load Structure: Load Structure speciﬁes the entry and/or exit charges, if any, applicable to investor transac Ɵons. For instance, if the NAV is Rs. 100 and the exit load is 1%, the redemp Ɵon price would be Rs.99 per unit.

NAV Details: NAV or Net Asset Value is the per-unit value of the fund a Ōer deduc Ɵng permissible expenses. It is calculated at the end of each business day and is the price at which investors enter or exit the fund.

Expense Ra Ɵo: Expense Ra Ɵo represents the annual expenses charged to the fund for managing and administering investments. These expenses are adjusted in the NAV and directly impact investor returns over Ɵme.
Risk Band: Risk Band indicates the level of risk associated with the fund as categorised by AMFI, helping investors assess suitability based on their risk appe Ɵte.
Disclaimer
Investments in specialized investment fund involves rela Ɵvely higher risk including potenƟal loss of capital, liquidity risk and market volaƟlity.
Please read all investment strategy related documents carefully before making the investment decision
